title: Blood, Sweat, and Pixels
creator: Jason Schreier
description: Ten reported accounts reconstruct difficult game productions, from solo development to large studios. The book covers delays, redesigns, technical problems, management, cancellation, and crunch through interviews with participants.
useful: It makes production risk legible through concrete projects and people rather than reducing development to success stories.
audience: Developers, students, and general readers seeking a reported view of how games are actually made.
learning: Recognize recurring production risks across different teams
Separate public launch narratives from development history
Discuss the human cost of scope, delay, and crunch
